public boolean canPartitionKSubsets(int[] nums, int k) {
    int total = Arrays.stream(nums).sum();
    if (total % k != 0) return false;
    int target = total / k;
    Arrays.sort(nums);
    reverseArray(nums); // to sort in descending order
    List<Integer> subsets = new ArrayList<>(Collections.nCopies(k, 0));
    return backtrack(nums, 0, subsets, target);
}

private boolean backtrack(int[] nums, int index, List<Integer> subsets, int target) {
    if (index == nums.length) {
        return true;
    }
    int num = nums[index];
    for (int i = 0; i < subsets.size(); i++) {
        if (subsets.get(i) + num <= target) {
            subsets.set(i, subsets.get(i) + num);
            if (backtrack(nums, index + 1, subsets, target)) {
                return true;
            }
            subsets.set(i, subsets.get(i) - num);
            // Optimization: if the subset was empty before adding, no need to try others
            if (subsets.get(i) == 0) {
                break;
            }
        }
    }
    return false;
}

private void reverseArray(int[] array) {
    int i = 0;
    int j = array.length - 1;
    while (i < j) {
        int temp = array[i];
        array[i] = array[j];
        array[j] = temp;
        i++;
        j--;
    }
}

class Solution {
    public boolean canPartitionKSubsets(int[] nums, int k) {
        int total = 0;
        for (int num : nums) {
            total += num;
        }
        if (total % k != 0) {
            return false;
        }
        int target = total / k;
        for (int num : nums) {
            if (num > target) {
                return false;
            }
        }
        Arrays.sort(nums);
        reverseArray(nums);
        List<Integer> subsets = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int i = 0; i < k; i++) {
            subsets.add(0);
        }
        return backtrack(nums, 0, subsets, target);
    }

    private boolean backtrack(int[] nums, int index, List<Integer> subsets, int target) {
        if (index == nums.length) {
            return true;
        }
        int num = nums[index];
        for (int i = 0; i < subsets.size(); i++) {
            if (subsets.get(i) + num <= target) {
                subsets.set(i, subsets.get(i) + num);
                if (backtrack(nums, index + 1, subsets, target)) {
                    return true;
                }
                subsets.set(i, subsets.get(i) - num);
                if (subsets.get(i) == 0) {
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
        return false;
    }

    private void reverseArray(int[] array) {
        int i = 0;
        int j = array.length - 1;
        while (i < j) {
            int temp = array[i];
            array[i] = array[j];
            array[j] = temp;
            i++;
            j--;
        }
    }
}

solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public boolean canPartitionKSubsets(int[] nums, int k) {
        int total = 0;
        for (int num : nums) {
            total += num;
        }
        if (total % k != 0) {
            return false;
        }
        int target = total / k;
        for (int num : nums) {
            if (num > target) {
                return false;
            }
        }
        Arrays.sort(nums);
        reverseArray(nums);
        List<Integer> subsets = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int i = 0; i < k; i++) {
            subsets.add(0);
        }
        return backtrack(nums, 0, subsets, target);
    }

    private boolean backtrack(int[] nums, int index, List<Integer> subsets, int target) {
        if (index == nums.length) {
            return true;
        }
        int num = nums[index];
        for (int i = 0; i < subsets.size(); i++) {
            if (subsets.get(i) + num <= target) {
                subsets.set(i, subsets.get(i) + num);
                if (backtrack(nums, index + 1, subsets, target)) {
                    return true;
                }
                subsets.set(i, subsets.get(i) - num);
                if (subsets.get(i) == 0) {
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
        return false;
    }

    private void reverseArray(int[] array) {
        int i = 0;
        int j = array.length - 1;
        while (i < j) {
            int temp = array[i];
            array[i] = array[j];
            array[j] = temp;
            i++;
            j--;
        }
    }
}
```

efficiency: MEDIUM

time complexity: O(k^N), where N is the number of elements. However, with optimizations like sorting and pruning, it's manageable for N up to 16.

space complexity: O(N), due to the recursion stack and the subsets list.

energy implications: MEDIUM

explanation: The solution uses a backtracking approach with pruning to efficiently explore possible partitions. Sorting the array in descending order helps in placing larger numbers first, reducing the search space. The algorithm checks if the total sum is divisible by k and if any number exceeds the target sum, which are quick checks to prune impossible cases early. The backtracking function attempts to place each number into each subset, ensuring the sum does not exceed the target, and uses optimizations to avoid redundant work.
